files.00_Overview.md: Add Learning Objectives section at the beginning. Add Key Takeaways summary at the end. The '3W Framework' is partially applied — enhance the 'ทำไม' (Why) callout boxes to explicitly state 'WHAT: Lid-Driven Cavity benchmark, WHY: Standard validation problem with reference data, HOW: Through OpenFOAM workflow steps' structure.
files.01_Introduction.md: Add Learning Objectives section. The 'ทำไมต้องเข้าใจ Workflow?' callout is good but needs explicit 3W labeling: 'WHAT: CFD workflow components, WHY: To debug simulations effectively, HOW: By understanding case structure'. The content overlaps significantly with 02_The_Workflow.md — consider merging or clearly differentiating: 01 should remain high-level conceptual, 02 should be detailed implementation.
files.02_The_Workflow.md: This is the most comprehensive file. Add Learning Objectives. 3W Framework is implicitly present but not explicit — convert section headers to follow 'WHAT: [component], WHY: [purpose], HOW: [implementation]' pattern. Good practical content but missing 'Learning Objectives' and 'Key Takeaways' sections. The sampling/probes content duplicates parts of 04_Step-by-Step_Tutorial.md — move detailed sampling examples to 04, keep high-level concepts here.
files.03_The_Lid-Driven_Cavity_Problem.md: Add Learning Objectives. This file has the best 3W Framework application with clear 'ทำไม' explanations throughout. However, the 'สมการควบคุม' section needs better WHAT/HOW structure. Add Key Takeaways summarizing: benchmark significance, physics features, Reynolds number effects, and mesh requirements. The grading syntax explanation is excellent — preserve this.
files.04_Step-by-Step_Tutorial.md: Add Learning Objectives at start ('Students will be able to run a complete cavity simulation'). Good procedural WHAT/HOW but weak on WHY for each step. Before each step, add a brief sentence explaining WHY this step matters in the overall workflow. The Step 10 content (sample functions) duplicates 02_The_Workflow.md — consolidate. Add troubleshooting section earlier or as collapsible notes within each step. Missing Key Takeaways.
files.05_Expected_Results.md: Add Learning Objectives. Good content on validation methodology. 3W Framework is partially present — enhance to explicitly state 'WHAT: Validation against Ghia reference, WHY: To verify simulation accuracy, HOW: Through centerline comparison and error metrics'. The Python script is valuable but positioned late — consider adding earlier with 'Prerequisites' warning. Add Key Takeaways summarizing acceptable error ranges and convergence criteria. Missing section on 'HOW to interpret discrepancies when they occur'.
files.06_Exercises.md: Add Learning Objectives ('Students will practice parameter variations and debugging'). This is the strongest file pedagogically — good progression from basic to advanced. Each exercise should have explicit Learning Outcome at the start of the problem statement. Add Key Takeaways section summarizing skills gained. Consider adding 'Estimated time for each exercise'. The turbulent flow exercise (8) is quite advanced — add prerequisite note or link to future module.

global_strategy: This folder has moderate redundancy between files 01-02 (workflow) and between files 02-04 (sampling/probes). REFACTOR PLAN: 1) Add standard Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways to ALL files. 2) Apply consistent 3W Framework (WHAT/WHY/HOW) to all major sections with explicit labels. 3) Restructure: 01 stays as high-level workflow concepts, 02 becomes detailed implementation reference, 04 focuses purely on step-by-step hands-on with minimal theory. 4) Move all sampling/probes detailed examples to 04 only. 5) Add cross-references between files where content overlaps. 6) Create a folder-level Key Takeaways document summarizing the complete module.
